sutta: once when the exalted 1 dwelt among the malas near their market town called uruveilla kappa and had 1 morning roped early taken bowl and cloaked entered uruguay la kapa for arms gone his rounds returned and eaten his meal he called the verbal ananda and said please wait here ananda while i am gone into mahavana for the midday rest i stop here for a moment mahavana means the great growth of the great forest and normally after eating their meals the monks go to secluded places and forests so here the buddha wanted to go into the great forest and venerable ananda replied yes lord and the exalted 1 entered mahavana for the midday rest and sat down at the foot of a tree now the good man takusa visited the verbal ananda saluted him and sat down at 1 side and so seated he said thus we householders reverend ananda a pleasure seekers pleasure for pleasure daughting pleasure man and being so it seems a real precipice to us this giving up of all yet i have heard reverend sir that in this dhamma vinaya the heart of every young monk leaps up at this giving up or renouncing becomes calm steadfast and inclined there to seeing it is the peace fair reverend sir is the difference in this between and the ordinary person i mean in this giving up ananda said this indeed is a topic for talking good men let us go and see the exalted 1 we will visit him and tell him the matter and as the exalted 1 explains so we will bear it in mind very well sir replied the good man to the verbal ananda and the verbal ananda who went with a good man tapusa and visited the exalted 1 saluted and sat down at 1 side and remember ananda told the exalted 1 all the good man had said and buddha said it is verily so and it is verily so when i was but a being awakening ananda and not wholly awakened before there was full awakenment i thought thus good is the giving up of all good it is to go apart but my mind leaped not up became not calm steadfast not inclined to this giving up though i saw it is the peace and i thought now what's the cause the reason then i thought the peril of pleasures is not seen by me is not made much of by me the advantage of this giving up is not 1 it's not enjoyed by me so my mind deeps not up becomes not calm steadfast not inclined to this giving up before i see it is the peace and i thought if seeing the parallel of pleasures i want to make much of it if winning the advantage of this giving up i were to enjoy it would surely happen that my mind would leap up become calm sit fast and incline to this giving up on seeing it to be the peace and just stop here for a moment so here the buddha is saying that even though when he first renounced her he thought now it is good to give up to go a partner but his mind did not really welcome it and did not become calm or inclined to this giving up and he realized that because the danger of sense uh pleasures was not seen by him so you can see here that it is necessary to see or to realize the danger of sensual pleasures to see that it leads to a greater craving if we indulge in sensual pleasures at leads to a greater craving for it because it cannot be satisfied and the buddha gave a parable in the majima nikaya about a butcher and a dog buddha said a hungry dog saw a butcher carving the meat from the bones so it went near the butcher and sat down there waiting for the butcher to give him a bone with the flesh butcher after carving up the meat threw him a big boner without any flesh and happily the dog carried this bone to 1 corner and started to chew on it to gnaw on it but in spite of chewing and knowing on the bone because there was no flesh the dog was still hungry and in the same way the buddha said when we indulge in sensual pleasures it is not something that is satisfying it is only for a moment it satisfies us but our desire becomes greater and sensual desires and the buddha said now it's like a disease in the magandhi suta in the majima nikaya the buddha gave a parable of a leper the buddha says this leper is disease the flesh is rotting so there is a terrible itch which makes him scratch his flesh the rotting flesh but the more he scratches the flesh the more it rots the more pus comes out blood comes up the more foul smelling it becomes but he has no choice because the itch is so terrible that he has to keep on scratching himself until more blood comes out more past comes out in spite of that he can't stand the itch which goes to his very bone so what does he do he goes to find a burning amber takes a burning amber and he burns his flesh and that satisfies the itch for a while and later he again has to scratch and find some other ember to burn his flesh but the more he scratches and burns his flesh the more the flesh loses blood more past develops more foul smelling it becomes and the worse his condition becomes it doesn't become better so in the same way when we indulge in sensual pleasures it is like this disease of the leper more they become more signal more craving is developed yeah so that is why sutas are very important when we listen to sutas and we listen to parables like these beautiful parables like this given by the buddha that we understand the nature of sense desires and we also understand the danger of sense desires and when we see it then we are more inclined to renouncing more inclined to giving up and the buddha continue and presently ananda on seeing the peril of danger i made much of it on winning the advantage i enjoyed it and my mind leaped up became calm steadfast and inclined to this giving up on seeing it was the peace and presently ananda aloof from sen's desires i entered and aboard in the first jhana but as i abort in this abiding thoughts and distractions of a sensual kind beset me and it was for me a disease just as some ill amounting to a disease might arise in some happy person and under even so such thoughts and distractions beset me and it was for me a disease stop here for a moment so here the buddha is saying when he tried to practice the janus and he entered the first jana very soon he was distracted now with sensual thoughts why was the buddha distracted with sensual thoughts of course before he renounced her as a lay person he was from a rich family and he was surrounded by a lot of slaves and these uh there were a lot of female slaves who attended to him like a deva and he said during the rainy season he would stay in this big mansion which his father built for him there were 3 seasons in india and every season he would stay in a different mansion and during the rainy season when it rained a lot for 4 months he stayed in this big mansion surrounded by all the female slaves with no men around so we enjoyed a lot of sensual pleasures and because of that when he became among and tried to practice these sensual memories of sensual desire came and disturbed him and when he entered the janus the thoughts of the sensual kindness distracted him and he came out of the jainas jana's states now that we have to practice continually to be able to abide along in them otherwise you enter a jana for a short while and you just come out of it automatically then the buddha continue then i thought what if i were to suppress applied and sustained thought and enter abide in this and abide in the second jhana the third the fourth jhana the sphere of infinite space of infinite consciousness of nothingness of neither perception or non-perception in the ending of perception and feeling and presently ananda passing wholly beyond this fear of neither perception or non-perception i entered and abroad in the ending of perception and feeling and i saw by wisdom that the sabbath were completely destroyed and so long and ananda as i attain not to emerge not from these 9 attainments of gradual abiding both forwards and backwards i realized not completely as 1 wholly awakened the full perfect awakening unsurpassed in the world with its gods maras and brahmas on earth with its recluses brahmins devas and men but when i attained to and emerged from these abiding such wise then wholly awakened i realized completely in the full awakening unsurpassed then knowledge and vision arose up within me the mind is released um and this release is unshakable this birth is final there is now no more becoming again that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so in this last partner the buddha is saying that he managed to control his mind and attain all the janas all the 4 rupa janas the 4 arupa janas and finally attain the ending of perception and feeling a cessation of perspiration destroyed the ask us and the buddha has said in this last partner so long and under as i attain not to emerge not from these 9 attainments of gradual abiding both forwards and backwards i realize not completely as 1 wholly awakened the full perfect awakening unsurpassed in the world so the buddha said as long as he did not attain these 9 gradual abidings namely the jhanas all the you can call them all the 9 janas both forwards and backwards he did not attain full awakening full enlightenment but after he had taken he had attained all the 9 jhanas forwards and backwards then oniya he attained the supreme enlightenment you know which shows the importance of attaining the janus but however it doesn't mean that psychic power you know some people confuse janas with psychic power they think that by attaining jhanas automatically 1 attains the psychic powers this is not so because we find like vulnerable sarikuta it is stated that whenever sarikuta has attained all the jhanas however verbal sarikuta has none of the psychic powers so you might not must not confuse the jhanas with psychic powers in exeter is
